question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

graph.answer:
**Tour**

This snapshot is client/hiredis-heavy rather than a single server-loop monolith. The clearest public entrypoints surfaced by the graph are:

- [src/redis-cli.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/redis-cli.c#L11176) `main`
- [src/redis-benchmark.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/redis-benchmark.c#L1711) `main`
- [deps/hiredis/examples/example-ae.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/examples/example-ae.c#L43) `main`
- [deps/hiredis/test.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/test.c#L2266) `main`

The central runtime shape in the tour is:

- `main` sets up state and wires callbacks.
- `redisAeAttach` binds hiredis to the `ae` event loop.
- `redisAsyncSetConnectCallback` and `redisAsyncSetDisconnectCallback` register lifecycle handlers.
- `redisAsyncCommand` queues the actual Redis work.
- `aeMain` drives the event loop and lets callbacks finish the job.

The most concrete flow the graph exposed is:

- [deps/hiredis/examples/example-ae.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/examples/example-ae.c#L43) `main`
- [deps/hiredis/adapters/ae.h](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/adapters/ae.h#L102) `redisAeAttach`
- [deps/hiredis/async.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/async.c#L252) `redisAsyncSetConnectCallback`
- [deps/hiredis/async.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/async.c#L260) `redisAsyncSetDisconnectCallback`
- [deps/hiredis/async.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/async.c#L985) `redisAsyncCommand`
- `aeMain`

The benchmark path is similar but adds command formatting and secure init before entering the loop:

- [src/redis-benchmark.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/src/redis-benchmark.c#L1711) `main`
- `redisFormatCommand`
- `redisFormatCommandArgv`
- `cliSecureInit`
- `aeMain`

Nearby code paths worth reading next:

- [deps/hiredis/examples/example-ae.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/examples/example-ae.c#L13) `getCallback`
- [deps/hiredis/examples/example-ae.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/examples/example-ae.c#L22) `connectCallback`
- [deps/hiredis/adapters/ae.h](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/adapters/ae.h#L102) `redisAeAttach`
- [deps/hiredis/async.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/async.c#L252) `redisAsyncSetConnectCallback`
- [deps/hiredis/async.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/async.c#L260) `redisAsyncSetDisconnectCallback`
- [deps/hiredis/async.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/async.c#L985) `redisAsyncCommand`
- [deps/hdr_histogram/hdr_histogram.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hdr_histogram/hdr_histogram.c#L49) `normalize_index.if` if your change touches benchmark/latency reporting

Tests and test-like code to read next:

- [deps/hiredis/test.c](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/hiredis/test.c#L2266) is the closest executable test harness in this tour.
- [deps/jemalloc/test/integration/cpp/basic.cpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/redis/deps/jemalloc/test/integration/cpp/basic.cpp#L3)

If your first behavior change is CLI-facing, start with `src/redis-cli.c`, then follow the hiredis async stack above. If you want, I can do a second pass focused just on one path, for example `redis-cli` command dispatch or the hiredis async callback chain.
